root_text.sn46.13:1.1: “Sūriyassa, bhikkhave, udayato etaṁ pubbaṅgamaṁ etaṁ pubbanimittaṁ, yadidaṁ—aruṇuggaṁ;
root_text.sn46.13:1.2: evameva kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno sattannaṁ bojjhaṅgānaṁ uppādāya etaṁ pubbaṅgamaṁ etaṁ pubbanimittaṁ, yadidaṁ—yonisomanasikāro.
root_text.sn46.13:1.3: Yonisomanasikārasampannassetaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno pāṭikaṅkhaṁ—satta bojjhaṅge bhāvessati, satta bojjhaṅge bahulīkarissati.
root_text.sn46.13:2.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu yonisomanasikārasampanno satta bojjhaṅge bhāveti, satta bojjhaṅge bahulīkaroti?
root_text.sn46.13:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu satisambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ …pe…
root_text.sn46.13:2.3: upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.
root_text.sn46.13:2.4: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu yonisomanasikārasampanno satta bojjhaṅge bhāveti, satta bojjhaṅge bahulīkarotī”ti.

translation_text.sn46.13:1.1: “Mendicants, the dawn is the forerunner and precursor of the sunrise.
translation_text.sn46.13:1.2: In the same way, for a mendicant rational application of mind is the forerunner and precursor of the arising of the seven awakening factors.
translation_text.sn46.13:1.3: A mendicant accomplished in rational application of mind can expect to develop and cultivate the seven awakening factors.
translation_text.sn46.13:2.1: And how does a mendicant with rational application of mind develop and cultivate the seven awakening factors?
translation_text.sn46.13:2.2: It’s when a mendicant develops the awakening factors of mindfulness, investigation of principles, energy, rapture, tranquility, immersion,
translation_text.sn46.13:2.3: and equanimity, which rely on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripen as letting go.
translation_text.sn46.13:2.4: That’s how a mendicant with rational application of mind develops and cultivates the seven awakening factors.”
